name: La Corchuela Park and Las Plateras Greenway Loop
name_original: Vía Verde de Las Planteras - vuelta desde Dos Hermanas
description: Mountain bike the moderate 25.7-mile La Corchuela Park and Las Plateras Greenway Loop, exploring diverse forests and marshlands.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Dos Hermanas offers diverse terrain within its flat countryside, which gently descends to the Guadalquivir Valley. The region features significant natural spaces like La Corchuela Periurban Park and Paraje Natural del Brazo del Este. These areas provide varied ecosystems, including holm oak groves, wild olive forests, and pine forests, suitable for off-road cycling. The network of routes includes both paved and unpaved segments, catering to different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Dos Hermanas The most popular mountain bike trail is La Corchuela Park and Las Plateras Greenway Loop, a 25.7 miles (41.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 58 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the diverse ecosystems of La Corchuela Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Dos Hermanas, Alcalá de Guadaíra, and Utrera Loop, a moderate 38.5 miles (61.9 km) path. This route connects multiple towns, offering varied scenery across the region. Local mountain bikers also love the End of the Canal de los Presos – Dos Hermanas Town Hall loop from Dos Hermanas, a 16.7 miles (26.8 km) trail leading through the Canal de los Presos area, often completed in about 1 hour 56 minutes. Mountain biking around Dos Hermanas is defined by periurban parks, the Guadalquivir Valley, and pine forests. The network offers options for easy rides as well as more challenging, unpaved adventures. The routes in Dos Hermanas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 900 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Dos Hermanas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Dos Hermanas?
A: Dos Hermanas offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 75 routes available on komoot. These routes cater to various preferences, from easy rides to more challenging adventures.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Dos Hermanas?
A: The terrain around Dos Hermanas is quite diverse. While generally known for its flat countryside, you'll find varied ecosystems within natural spaces like La Corchuela Periurban Park and Paraje Natural del Brazo del Este. Expect a mix of paved and unpaved segments, including holm oak groves, wild olive forests, and pine forests, offering options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families or beginners in Dos Hermanas?
A: Yes, Dos Hermanas has trails suitable for families and beginners. Approximately 15 of the routes are classified as easy, offering gentle rides through the region's natural beauty. An example of an easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38208915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;End of the Canal de los Presos – Dos Hermanas Town Hall loop from Dos Hermanas&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 16.7 miles long.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Dos Hermanas?
A: Dos Hermanas benefits from a Mediterranean climate with mild winters and warm summers, making it suitable for mountain biking year-round. However, for the most comfortable experience, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails pass through significant natural areas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990784886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Corchuela Park and Las Plateras Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; explores the diverse ecosystems of La Corchuela Periurban Park, known for its holm oak groves and wild olive forests. You might also encounter areas near the Guadalquivir Valley and pine forests, offering scenic views.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. While many routes are moderate, there are 11 routes classified as difficult for those seeking a greater challenge. These routes often feature more significant elevation changes and technical sections. An example of a difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31423590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alamillo Bridge – San Juan Bridge loop from Cantaelgallo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers a substantial distance with considerable elevation gain.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Dos Hermanas?
A: The mountain bike trails in Dos Hermanas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the accessibility of routes, and the opportunity to explore periurban parks, the Guadalquivir Valley, and pine forests.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in Dos Hermanas?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Dos Hermanas are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990803744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dos Hermanas, Alcalá de Guadaíra, and Utrera Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate circular path connecting multiple towns, offering varied scenery.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink near the mountain bike routes?
A: While specific cafes directly on every trail are not guaranteed, many routes are easily accessible from Dos Hermanas and surrounding towns. This means you'll likely find various establishments for refreshments before or after your ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32035034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alpériz Palace – Moli Tapas loop from Dos Hermanas&lt;/a&gt; starts and ends near urban areas where amenities are available.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions nearby that I can visit after my ride?
A: Yes, Dos Hermanas is close to several notable attractions. You can explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1055590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Alcázar of Seville&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2456715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marchenilla Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Other significant landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/215404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seville Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; and the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/112737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza of Spain&lt;/a&gt;, all within a reasonable distance.

Q: Is parking generally available near the trailheads in Dos Hermanas?
A: Given that many routes are accessible from Dos Hermanas and its periurban parks, parking is generally available in designated areas within or near these parks and towns. It's advisable to check specific starting points for the most convenient parking options.
tips: It is a nice shady stretch next to the road that will give you a bit of respite if you are pedaling in summer during hours with a lot of sunlight.

The Parque Periurbano La Corchuela is a beautiful nature reserve located between Dos Hermanas and Seville, Spain. The park covers approximately 85 hectares and offers a diverse natural environment with different ecosystems, including a valley of encinas (oaks) in the south and a forest of wild olive trees in the north. The park is a popular place for recreation and nature experience, with several hiking trails and cycle routes that lead through the beautiful landscapes. Visitors can enjoy the peace and nature, and there are also educational attractions such as information panels that help identify different plant and animal species.

Nice stretch of the Metropolitan Green Corridor.
You can pedal along the path or along the wide track, where sometimes a car passes.
This is a very interesting option to connect the Sevillian aljarafe with the city, making use of the Coria barge that will cross you over the Guadalquivir river.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of mountain biking the La Corchuela Park and Las Plateras Greenway Loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is generally considered to have a moderate difficulty. The routes within Parque Periurbano de La Corchuela are accessible, making it suitable for a range of skill levels without being overly technical.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a mix of surfaces. The park itself features diverse natural environments, including valleys of encinas (oaks), wild olive tree forests, and ancient pine forests. Expect picturesque landscapes with varied flora. Some sections, like those on the Metropolitan Green Corridor, may have paved sections with 'slurry' treatment for landscape integration, while others will be more natural trail surfaces.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several interesting areas. You'll ride through Parque Periurbano de La Corchuela, known for its natural beauty and recreational facilities. You'll also experience a Shaded Path Section, offering a pleasant break from the sun, and parts of the Metropolitan Green Corridor of Seville. The area also has historical significance, with old cattle trails and the Canal de Los Presos infrastructure.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is typically during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May), to avoid the intense summer heat of Andalusia. Spring offers pleasant temperatures and blooming flora, while autumn provides comfortable riding conditions.

faqs.question: Is the La Corchuela Park and Las Plateras Greenway Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on trails within La Corchuela Park and along greenways. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural park areas, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for La Corchuela Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or specific permits required for general access to Parque Periurbano de La Corchuela or for mountain biking on the greenways. It's a public peri-urban natural reserve designed for recreation.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the La Corchuela Park and Las Plateras Greenway Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact loop aren't detailed, La Corchuela Park is well-equipped for visitors. You can typically find parking facilities near the main entrances of Parque Periurbano de La Corchuela, which serves as a good starting point for the loop. Access from Dos Hermanas is also common.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll ride along parts of the Colada de Las Plateras, various sections of the Vía Verde, and even small portions of the Vía Augusta de Cádiz a Sevilla and the Transandalus.

faqs.question: What kind of natural scenery and wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The park is known for its diverse natural environment, including valleys of encinas, wild olive tree forests, and an important ancient pine forest. You can expect to see varied flora and, depending on the time of day and season, local birdlife and small mammals typical of Mediterranean scrubland and forest ecosystems.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trail, like cafes or accommodation?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is primarily natural, being a peri-urban park near Dos Hermanas and Seville means you'll find amenities relatively close by. Within Parque Periurbano de La Corchuela, there are recreational facilities, and the nearby towns offer cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options.

faqs.question: Is the La Corchuela Park and Las Plateras Greenway Loop suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and accessible terrain, many sections of this loop, particularly within Parque Periurbano de La Corchuela, can be suitable for families. The park also hosts 'Aventura Sevilla,' an adventure park with tree-top circuits, which could be an additional attraction for families.
